name: Classic English Muffins
method: Throughout the mixing bowl fitted with the dough hook,
combine flour, sugar, and salt. Whisk together the milk and
yeast in a small dish or measuring cup. Toss the dry ingredients
with the milk mixture, butter, and egg. 15 minutes of mixing on
medium-high speed until dough forms a ball and is glossy and
elastic. The dough will remain slightly sticky.
Spray a large mixing basin with cooking spray, then scrape the
dough into it—cover and chill for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Remove dough from the fridge and set aside for 1 hour at room
temperature.
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Line two large
baking sheets with parchment paper and sprinkle with cornmeal.
Prepare a work surface by lightly flouring it and dividing the
dough into 15 equal pieces. Pinch the seams together and roll
each element on the counter with a cupped palm to form a
smooth ball. Place on prepared baking pans. Sprinkle a little
cornmeal on top of each ball. Cover loosely and set aside for 45
minutes at room temperature.
Preheat a medium skillet over low heat, ideally cast iron. Flatten
each ball to 1" thick by lightly pressing it down. Cook in
batches of 3 to 4 until the bottoms are golden brown,
approximately 7 minutes. Flip the dough over and push it down
again. Cook for a further 5–7 minutes, or until golden. Rep with
the rest of the dough.
Return muffins to a large baking sheet and bake for 10 minutes,
or until cooked through.
Cut open muffins with a fork and serve warm.
